summary: This cluster focuses on the taxonomic revision and morphological characterization of freshwater diatoms, specifically the genus *Pseudostaurosiropsis*, using scanning electron microscopy (SEM) to resolve species-level ambiguities. Researchers described two new species from the United States, distinguished by lanceolate valves with sub-rostrate apices, dentate spines, and a unique rotae configuration, which challenged prior generic boundaries. Key findings emphasized that the structural features of the *rotae* and *olae* are critical for differentiating these taxa from closely related genera. The work underscores the necessity of integrating high-resolution morphological data with broader taxonomic frameworks to achieve accurate classification within this group.
